chat-service.description: creates a chat; user_id will be inferred from the headers in the request and will be used to check if the user exists (via user-db) and then for creating a chat in the chat-db. title will default to 'new chat'

downstreamDescriptions.user-db: checks that the X-User-Id header refers to an existing user before creating a chat
downstreamDescriptions.chat-db: inserts the new chat row (user_id from the header, title defaults to 'new chat')

history.description: creates a chat; user_id will be inferred from the headers in the request and will be used to check if the user exists (via user-db) and then for creating a chat in the chat-db. title will default to 'new chat'

chat-service.description: get chats for user; user_id is from header; chats should be ordered by updated_at; cursor is start index (default to 0) and maxChats is how many to query (default to 10)

downstreamDescriptions.chat-db: reads the caller's chat rows (user_id from the X-User-Id header) ordered by updated_at, paged with cursor as offset and maxChats as limit

history.description: get chats for user; user_id is from header; chats should be ordered by updated_at; cursor is start index (default to 0) and maxChats is how many to query (default to 10)

chat-service.description:
verify user exists in user_id via user_id in auth header. if not then error
verify chat exists via chat_id and user_id matchest up. if not then error
user_msg_id = snowflake_id
assistant_msg_id = snowflake_id
write in single transaction [
Message(id: user_msg_id, content: request.content, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id), role: 'user')
UserMessage(id: assistant_msg_id, message: user_msg_id, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id))
]
then relay the llm-worker's generated tokens as SSE. The worker XADDs TYPED entries to redis stream tokens:{assistant_msg_id} on llm-worker-stream — each entry is {type: "token"|"done"|"error", text: string}. Read with XREAD BLOCK 5000 from id "0" and dispatch on type (NEVER on the text):
- type=="token": yield data: {"token": <text>}   # one a-z char
- type=="done": yield data: {"done": true, "last": <text>} and close — <text> is the worker's configurable LAST_TOKEN marker (etcd /config/app-settings/, default "DONE"), informational only
- type=="error": yield data: {"error": <text>} and close — the generation aborted
A timed-out XREAD sends an SSE keepalive comment and blocks again; the whole stream is hard-capped at 120s so the request always terminates. (The worker also XADDs {run_id: assistant_msg_id} to the runs:started stream on every accepted AddPrompt — that announcement drives the persistence readers, not this endpoint.)

downstreamDescriptions.user-db: verifies the X-User-Id header refers to an existing user before accepting the message
downstreamDescriptions.chat-db: verifies the chat belongs to the user, then inserts the user Message + UserMessage rows in one transaction (the user_message INSERT is what CDC captures to drive inference)
downstreamDescriptions.llm-worker-stream: block-reads the worker's typed token stream (tokens:<assistant_msg_id>, entries {type: token|done|error, text}) and relays each token to the caller as SSE, closing on the done/error entry (keyed off type, never the text)

history.description:
verify user exists in user_id via user_id in auth header. if not then error
verify chat exists via chat_id and user_id matchest up. if not then error
user_msg_id = snowflake_id
assistant_msg_id = snowflake_id
write in single transaction [
Message(id: user_msg_id, content: request.content, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id), role: 'user')
UserMessage(id: assistant_msg_id, message: user_msg_id, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id))
]
then read from redis stream and send sse events to caller of this method. do something like this (you will need to check what the output is from the llm worker and redis stream is in no way am i saying the below is correct)
last_token = env.LAST_TOKEN (from app-settings config)
last_id = "0"
while True:
    result = XREAD BLOCK 5000 STREAMS stream:{request_id} last_id
    if result:
        for entry_id, fields in result:
            token = fields["token"]
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'token': token})}\n\n"   # push down SSE
            last_id = entry_id
            if token == last_token:
                return
    else: timed out, loop and block again (lets you send keepalives)

verify user exists in user_id via user_id in auth header. if not then error
verify chat exists via chat_id and user_id matchest up. if not then error
user_msg_id = snowflake_id
assistant_msg_id = snowflake_id
write in single transaction [
Message(id: user_msg_id, content: request.content, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id), role: 'user')
UserMessage(id: assistant_msg_id, message: user_msg_id, chat: Chat(id: request.chat_id))
]
then relay the llm-worker's generated tokens as SSE. The worker XADDs TYPED entries to redis stream tokens:{assistant_msg_id} on llm-worker-stream — each entry is {type: "token"|"done"|"error", text: string}. Read with XREAD BLOCK 5000 from id "0" and dispatch on type (NEVER on the text):
- type=="token": yield data: {"token": <text>}   # one a-z char
- type=="done": yield data: {"done": true, "last": <text>} and close — <text> is the worker's configurable LAST_TOKEN marker (etcd /config/app-settings/, default "DONE"), informational only
- type=="error": yield data: {"error": <text>} and close — the generation aborted
A timed-out XREAD sends an SSE keepalive comment and blocks again; the whole stream is hard-capped at 120s so the request always terminates. (The worker also XADDs {run_id: assistant_msg_id} to the runs:started stream on every accepted AddPrompt — that announcement drives the persistence readers, not this endpoint.)
